tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
26th May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iranian negotiators signaled openness to transferring the country’s highly enriched uranium stockpile to China, seeking Beijing’s security guarantees as part of a potential nuclear agreement with the US.

● Partial restoration of internet connectivity began in Iran on day 88 of near-total blackout — the longest nationwide internet shutdown in the country’s history.

● UKMTO reported an incident 60 nautical miles east of Muscat, Oman. A vessel suffered an external explosion on the port side near the waterline, with some bunker fuel discharged into the sea. The crew is safe.

● Iranian Foreign Ministry condemned recent US actions as a “grave violation of the ceasefire,” accusing Washington of bad faith and warning of consequences.

● The IDF dismantled ~11km of underground tunnel routes east of the Yellow Line in the Beit Hanoun area in northern Gaza, a former Hamas terrorist stronghold.

● The US Navy resumed escorting commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz under the revived “Project Freedom.” A Greek supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of crude was the first vessel escorted.

● Trump urged Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, and Jordan to join the Abraham Accords and normalize ties with Israel. Pakistan rejected the invitation.

END OF THE BRIEF

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
25th May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iranian negotiators are demanding immediate access to $12 billion in frozen Qatari assets as a non-negotiable precondition for any further talks with the United States.

● The Qatari tanker AL RAYYAN successfully exited the Strait of Hormuz en route to China after loading at Ras Laffan. A second vessel, FUWAIRIT, is also currently transiting the strait heading to Pakistan.

● Iran’s FM announced plans to impose an “environmental tax” on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf, and Sea of Oman, explicitly rejecting the term “toll.”

● The VLCC IDEMITSU MARU docked at a pier off Chita City, Japan, carrying approximately 2 million barrels of Saudi crude - one month after successfully passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

● A senior US administration official confirmed that an emerging proposal includes a 60-day ceasefire extension to allow complete de-mining and formal reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. However, the White House stressed that no formal agreement has been signed yet.

END OF THE BRIEF

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: बीकानेर में अमित शाह की हाई-लेवल सुरक्षा बैठक संपन्न, सीमा सुरक्षा से लेकर आतंकवाद, नार्कोटिक्स और खुफिया समन्वय पर मंथन; BSF मुख्यालय में देश की शीर्ष सुरक्षा एजेंसियों के साथ चली मैराथन रणनीतिक बैठक के बाद दिल्ली रवाना हुए केंद्रीय गृहमंत्री

Union Home Minister Amit Shah concluded a high-level strategic security review meeting at the BSF Headquarters in Bikaner, Rajasthan, bringing together India’s top security, intelligence, border management, and administrative leadership. The closed-door meeting witnessed the presence of BSF Director General Praveen Kumar, Union Home Secretary Govind Mohan, Intelligence Bureau Director Tapan Kumar Deka, NCB Director IPS Anurag Garg, IG BSF Rajasthan Frontier M. L. Garg, DIG BSF Ajay Luthra, Rajasthan DGP Rajeev Sharma and Chief Secretary V. Srinivas among several senior officials.

The marathon security deliberations reportedly focused on border security preparedness, anti-infiltration measures, counter-terror coordination, narcotics trafficking routes, intelligence sharing mechanisms, and administrative preparedness across Rajasthan’s border districts. Senior district collectors, divisional commissioners, SPs from multiple border districts, and top anti-crime officials also attended the strategic review.

Following the meeting, Amit Shah departed from the BSF Headquarters to Nal Air Force Station before leaving for Delhi via a special aircraft. Rajasthan Chief Minister Bhajan Lal Sharma is also scheduled to depart from Nal Airport. The visit is being seen as a major signal of the Centre’s heightened focus on western border security, internal intelligence coordination, and anti-terror preparedness in the desert sector.

tweets.title:
BREAKING: Home Minister Announces High-Level Panel on Demographic Change

Union Home Minister Amit Shah has announced the formation of a high-level committee to examine “unnatural demographic changes” in India, citing concerns linked to national security, sovereignty, and social stability. 

According to Shah, the panel will study demographic shifts allegedly caused by illegal infiltration and other factors, following a broader “Demography Mission” announced by PM Narendra Modi in 2025. 

The committee is expected to:

- Examine patterns of demographic change
- Assess security implications, especially in border regions
- Recommend measures to address illegal infiltration and related challenges 

The announcement comes amid the government’s push for a “smart border” project along the Pakistan and Bangladesh frontiers to strengthen surveillance and prevent illegal crossings.

tweets.title:
Russia’s upgraded Ka-226T helicopter has successfully completed its maiden flight with the new indigenous VK-650V engine.

Key highlights:

1. Earlier powered by French Safran Arrius 2G1 engines
2. Now equipped with Russia’s domestically developed VK-650V turboshaft engine
3. Flight tests included taxiing, stable hovering, and aerial maneuvering
3. Major step towards reducing dependence on Western aerospace components amid sanctions
4. The Ka-226T is also linked to the India-Russia joint helicopter production program (Was pushed on Back burner earlier).

Yesterday’s operation in Gulmarg was nothing short of heroic. Tourists stranded in the Gondola at nearly 11,000 feet after a major technical snag were rescued in extremely difficult conditions, amid harsh weather and challenging terrain. J&K Police and its SDRF wing, along with other security forces, carried out a highly professional and courageous rescue operation. LG J&K @OfficeOfLGJandK and DGP @JmuKmrPolice continuously monitored the situation, while DGP J&K Shri Nalin Prabhat himself, along with senior leadership of J&K Police, rushed to the site and physically supervised the entire operation till the safety of every stranded tourist was ensured.

Such operations once again prove that J&K Police is not only fighting terrorism on one front, but is also always standing shoulder to shoulder with the people during emergencies and disasters. Salute to every officer, jawan and rescue personnel involved in this operation. True heroes. 🫡
